text:
Once upon a time in a quiet village lived a rabbit named Olive. Olive loved reading tales of adventure, so she spent every spare moment flipping through the big owl-books. Her favorite was "The Adventures of Owlio."

One lazy afternoon, Olive glanced out her window. There lay a big, brown owl perched atop an oak. Olive felt an immediate connection. They eyed each other.

"You always pore over those books," Olive said.

The owl hooted softly. "What would you read?"

Olive sighed. "Just classics."

That night owlflies sang. Olive imagined Owlio discovering ancient maps.

Next day, Olive stole Owlio's favorites.

Owlcliffe!

Olive enjoyed them, and Owlio didn't care.

A year passed. Olive came home late.

"You've read everything!"

"No. I've made my own!"

They shared stories.

That night an owl flew over Owlcliffe.

You wouldn't believe what saw.

A beautiful songbird.

Owlcliffe
